title: Local government: emergency response services: use of languages other than English.
subject: Localgovernment
emergencyresponseservices
useoflanguagesotherthanEnglish

abstracts.abstract:
Existing law requires, in the event of an emergency within the jurisdiction of a local agency that provides emergency response services and that serves a population within which 5% or more of the people speak English less than "very well," according to American Community Survey data, and jointly speak a language other than English, that the local agency provide information related to the emergency in English and in all languages spoken jointly by the 5% or more of the population that speaks English less than "very well," as specified.

This bill would revise these provisions to require the local agency to provide information related to the emergency in English and in each language spoken jointly by 5% or more of the population that speaks English less than "very well."

actions.description: From printer. May be heard in committee March 22.
From committee chair, with author's amendments: Amend, and re-refer to Com. on E.M. Read second time and amended.

extras.impact_clause: An act to amend Section 7299.7 of the Government Code, relating to local government.
